payload.ABOUT_SCREEN.TITLE: About UTILITYCO
payload.ABOUT_SCREEN.WHO_WE_ARE:
UTILITYCO's app works as a personal energy advisor by providing customers with relevant, timely and personal insights related to their electricity account including electricity usage and power outage information. The appliance specific electricity usage profiles allow customers to begin to understand where, when and how much electricity they are using and how they can better manage their consumption. Comparisons to similar homes in the neighbourhood provide customers with a benchmark for behavioural change.

Hydro Ottawa is an electricity distribution company that safely and reliably delivers electricity to more than 325,000 customers in the City of Ottawa and the Village of Casselman. The essence of Hydro Ottawa’s business strategy is to put the customer at the centre of everything we do, and that starts with understanding what's important to our customers in an evolving energy landscape. We strive to create a more effortless and engaging customer experience, and to provide a broad range of services to help our customers manage their energy consumption and costs.

payload.DISAGG101.HEADLINE: Appliance Itemization Explained
payload.DISAGG101.SOLAR.ANSWER_1: Solar panels absorb energy from the sun and convert it to direct current (DC) electricity. Most homes and businesses run on alternating current (AC) electricity. The DC electricity is passed through an inverter to convert it to AC electricity, which is then used or sent back to the grid.
payload.DISAGG101.SOLAR.ANSWER_2: It is a meter that can measure the flow of electricity in two ways.  It measures how much energy comes from the utility (kWh delivered) when the solar system is not producing electricity like at night. It also measures the difference between what is generated by the solar system and the customers load. It does NOT measure the solar systems total production.  As the system is producing electricity, this energy is used to meet the customer's electric requirements. If more energy is produced than used this electricity is fed to the utility. This is typically during daylight hours. The bi-directional meter records this. At night when the solar is not producing the customer is using electricity from utility. If the customer produces more that they use they will see a credit on their energy statement. If the customer uses more than they produce they are charged for this power.
payload.DISAGG101.SOLAR.ANSWER_3: A bi-directional meter is a meter that can measure the flow of electricity in two directions - flowing from the utility's power grid to a customer and flowing from a customer's home or business back to the power grid.
payload.DISAGG101.SOLAR.QUESTION_1: How do solar panels generate electricity?

payload.DISAGG101_AIC.ALWAYS_ON.DESC: Always On includes plugged-in devices such as personal computers, monitors, printers, stereos, and televisions that consume energy even when they're turned off.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.COOKING.DESC: Cooking and food preparation appliances that consume significant electricity can be found in this category, including oven, stove, toaster, and toaster oven.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.COOLING.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of a natural gas powered dryer. Note: If you use a natural gas dryer and your consumption does not appear in this category, please ensure you’ve completed the Home Profile Survey.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.ELECTRIC_VEHICLE.DESC: This category includes the aggregate usage of all EVs at the home.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.ELECTRIC_WATER_HEATING.DES: Water Heating includes all whole-home water heaters(tanked, tankless, and heat pumps) as well as specific-use water heaters(e.g. electric showers).
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.ENTERTAINMENT.DESC: Entertainment is comprised of two main appliance types: consumer electronics (TV, DVR, game console, stereo) and home computing (desktop, monitor, printer, modem).
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.HEADLINE: Appliance Itemization Categories
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.LAUNDRY.DESC: Laundry consists of "wet washing" electric appliances, such as dishwasher, washing machine, and dryer.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.LIGHTING.DESC: This includes all lights in the home, from efficient LED bulbs to inefficient halogen bulbs, and everything in between.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.OTHER.DESC: These are appliances that are too small or infrequently used to be categorized separately. This includes mobile phones, vacuum cleaners, fans, hair dryers, and curling irons.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.POOL_PUMP.DESC: Pool pumps have motors that keep your pool water filtered and clean year-round.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.REFRIGERATION.DESC: Refrigeration includes the aggregate usage of refrigerators and freezers in the home.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.SPACE_HEATING.DESC: Electric space heating includes many different types of heaters: radiant, convection, fan, storage, underfloor, heat pumps, and the electric fan component of gas heating.
payload.DISAGG101_AIC.SUB_HEADLINE: We itemize your usage into 6 major appliance categories. You can track the usage of every single category over the course of the year and see how they change based on season, weather and your natural gas consumption behavior. We display categories that consume large amounts of natural gas, like Heating, Water Heating, Cooking, and Laundry, based on the usage from your meter. Thus, through a combination of your actual usage and our artificial intelligence, we can compose a holistic picture of your total natural gas consumption.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.COOKING.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of any cooking appliance in your home that consumes natural gas, such as a natural gas stove or oven.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.HEATING.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of any heating appliance in your home that consumes natural gas, such as central gas furnaces.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.LAUNDRY.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of a natural gas powered dryer. Note: If you use a natural gas dryer and your consumption does not appear in this category, please ensure you’ve completed the Home Profile Survey.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.OTHER.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of other natural gas appliances in your home, that may be used infrequently or are too small to be categorized separately, such as outdoor grills and gas lamps.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.POOL_HEATING.DESC: Consumption in this category represents the usage of a gas-based pool heater. Note: If you own a gas-based pool heater and your consumption doesn't appear in this category, please ensure you have updated your home profile.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.SUB_HEADLINE: We itemize your usage into 4 major appliance categories. You can track the usage of every single category over the course of the year and see how they change based on season, weather and your behavior. We display categories that consume large amounts of gas, like Heating, Water Heating, Cooking, and Laundry, based on the usage from your meter. Thus, through a combination of your actual usage and our artificial intelligence, we can compose a holistic picture of your total energy consumption.
payload.DISAGG101_AICGAS.WATER_HEATING.DESC: Consumption in this category represents usage of any water heating appliance in your home that consumes natural gas, such as whole-home gas water heaters (storage or tankless).
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.ANSWER_1: We send email alerts and notifications on a regular basis to provide personalized information to help you better understand your natural gas usage and manage costs.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.ANSWER_2: Yes, you can opt-out of email alerts or change your preferences any time by clicking “Unsubscribe” at the bottom of any of the emails you have received. This link will take you to your preferences page and allow you to customize which alerts you receive.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.ANSWER_3:
Email alerts consist of two types:
The Monthly Summary email captures how much natural gas you used during your last billing period, provides insight into natural gas usage by appliance, and includes tips on how to save.
The Similar Home Comparison email shows how your usage compares with similar homes in your area.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.ANSWER_4: Any personal information or collected data used for this service is done in accordance with Energy CO’s Privacy Policy.

payload.DISAGG101_AIE.QUESTION_4: How is my information being used?
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.ANSWER_1: Not really. Each appliance uses electricity in a unique manner - think of it like an appliance fingerprint. We detect and extract these "fingerprints" and convert the data into useful insights and recommendations.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.ANSWER_2: The details of net metering from your utility will tell us how much you imported and exported to the grid in a given period. We use our patented algorithm to then estimate your solar generation and that helps us to determine your gross household consumption (grid consumption + solar consumption). We disaggregate this gross consumption to provide accurate 100% itemization.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.ANSWER_3: Don't worry, we've got you covered. The insights we derive from each appliance in your home will change according to your actual usage patterns. So whether you go on vacation, buy a new appliance, or have a new addition to the family, we'll make sure you've got the most accurate and relevant appliance usage information at your fingertips.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.ANSWER_4: It sure is! Our disaggregation technology and home assessment survey shows how much you actually use per appliance, not how much a "typical" home should use. You're not a statistic; we won't treat you like one.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.ANSWER_5: We itemize your usage into 12 major appliance categories. You can track the usage of every single category over the course of the year and see how they change based on season, weather and your behavior. We display categories that consume a large amount of electricity, like Always On (baseload), Heating, Cooling and Pool Pump, based on the usage from your meter. We then take this usage to infer and estimate how much your other appliance categories, like laundry, cooking, lighting and entertainment, are consuming. Thus, through a combination of your actual usage and our rule-based model, we can compose a holistic picture of your total energy consumption.
payload.DISAGG101_AIE.SOLAR.QUESTION_2: How does disaggregation work with solar?

payload.DISAGG101_AIE.TEASER_TEXT.SOLAR: Appliance itemization is powered by disaggregation… a process that takes the energy usage data from your meter and uses software algorithms to identify the individual appliances that are actually using the energy.

payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_1: Personalized usage alerts are created using information from your natural gas meter, and based on your home type, size, and heating appliances gathered from public sources.  These information are sometimes out of date such as recent renovation made to your home. Any additional information that you choose to provide such as  the Home Profile Survey could be used to improve the content of your usage alerts.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_2: Appliance Breakdown is the process of using artificial intelligence (AI) to transform energy usage data from your meter in order to identify and track the energy consumption of individual natural gas appliances.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_3: Each appliance uses natural gas in a unique manner - think of it like an appliance fingerprint. We detect and extract these "fingerprints" and convert the data into useful insights and recommendations.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_4: This survey is designed to gather additional information regarding your home type and size. Understanding more about your home and the appliances you own, our AI knows what to look for and what to ignore as it works to breakdown your natural gas usage and create your appliance itemization.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_5: The Similar Home Comparison is an insight that allows the benchmarking of your natural gas usage against other homes with similar size, type, geographical location, climate, etc.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_6: Your actual usage pattern and your usage data will provide us insights. Whether you go on vacation, buy a new appliance, or have a new addition to the family, we'll make sure you've got the most accurate and relevant natural gas usage information at your fingertips.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_7: The AI driving our technology identifies which appliances you use and how much natural gas each consumes. It then presents personalized energy savings tips to you, along with a summary of your usage, in email alerts as described above.
payload.DISAGG101_HER.ANSWER_8: If you have any additional questions, contact NMGC. at energy.efficiency@nmgco.com or call (505) 697-3611.

- meter or billing data may not be available after a billing cycle has ended.
- consumption levels may be below a detectable threshold.
If you do not see your disaggregation breakdown this month, please check back after your next billing cycle has completed.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_2: See something in your appliance breakdown such as a washer/dryer or electric water heater that you don’t have in your home? It may be estimated from a rules based model. You can help improve the accuracy of your appliance breakdown by completing your Home Survey.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_3: Most people are eager to reduce their energy use and save money, but they're not always sure how to do so. The myEnergy Insights dashboard is designed to empower customers with information about their energy use and provide insights and choices to help them conserve and more efficiently use electricity to save on energy costs, manage their demand and move to cleaner energy sources.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_4: It's understandable that some people may have life or housing circumstances that make it difficult to reduce their home's energy use. We aim to provide a wide range of ideas that can address the various needs of our customers and their homes. We encourage you to find out which tips are best for you and your family. In some cases, you may not be able to realize substantial savings, but we've found that most people can realize at least some savings by using the various energy saving tips we provide.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_5: View our tips on the "My Recommendations" page of your myEnergy Insights dashboard. You can also visit www.hydroone.com/saving-money-and-energy to find additional information about savings energy at home and at work.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_6: The graph on the Home page of your myEnergy Insights Dashboard, as well as the Appliance, Monthly Summary and Bill Analysis pages under the Energy Insights tab should match your usage and total bill amounts for your most recent billing period.
payload.DISAGG101_OTHER.ANSWER_7: If you received a bill that required a correction to your usage or charges, the corrected values will appear on Your Home Energy Dashboard within two weeks of your corrected bill.

payload.FAQ_VIDEO_TRANSCRIPT_SCRIPT: We first see an animation of a man standing in his kitchen holding a piece of paper. The paper is revealed to show a list of energy usage in different categories (Cooling, Always On, Cooking, Heating, Laundry, Lighting, Entertainment, Refrigeration). An animation is shown of artificial intelligence differentiating dogs from cats. Quick shot of multiple household appliances including a refrigerator, oven, and washing machine. Animations show how washing machines and refrigerators work while a graph displaying their energy usage over time is pictured to the right. We see how artificial intelligence can identify appliances and how much energy they use after receiving a meter reading from a home. We cut back to the man standing in his kitchen holding the paper. He smiles and the video ends.

(c) (C) (r) (R) (tm) (TM) (p) (P) +-

test.. test... test..... test?..... test!....

!!!!!! ???? ,,  -- ---

"Smartypants, double quotes" and 'single quotes'


## Emphasis

**This is bold text**

__This is bold text__

*This is italic text*

_This is italic text_

~~Strikethrough~~


## Blockquotes


> Blockquotes can also be nested...
>> ...by using additional greater-than signs right next to each other...
> > > ...or with spaces between arrows.


## Lists

Unordered

+ Create a list by starting a line with '+', '-', or '*'
+ Sub-lists are made by indenting 2 spaces:
  - Marker character change forces new list start:
    * Ac tristique libero volutpat at
    + Facilisis in pretium nisl aliquet
    - Nulla volutpat aliquam velit
+ Very easy!

Ordered

1. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
2. Consectetur adipiscing elit
3. Integer molestie lorem at massa


1. You can use sequential numbers...
1. ...or keep all the numbers as '1.'

Start numbering with offset:

57. foo
1. bar


## Code

Inline 'code'

Indented code

    // Some comments
    line 1 of code
    line 2 of code
    line 3 of code
